problem_statement: Implement rle_encode(text) in py/rle.py. Scan the string into maximal runs of one character: a run of length one emits just the character, while longer runs emit the decimal count followed by the character (twelve a's give "12a"). Escape rule: when the run's character is itself a digit 0-9 or a backslash, prefix it with a single backslash so decoding stays unambiguous — "1222" encodes to backslash-1, 3, backslash-2. Empty input returns the empty string; non-str input raises TypeError. Verify with python3 -m pytest -q tests/py/test_rle*.py.
